ddls:
CREATE TABLE "titles" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each title */,
  "title_name" TEXT /* Name of the title */,
  "title_type" TEXT /* Type of title (e.g., movie, TV show, documentary) */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the title (e.g., action, comedy, drama) */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Release year of the title */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating of the title (e.g., PG-13, R, TV-MA) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the title */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the title (minutes for movies, average episode length for TV shows) */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the directors table */,
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the producers table */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* The production company responsible for the title */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* The country where the title was produced */,
  "budget" TEXT /* The budget of the title */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* The revenue generated by the title */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL to the trailer of the title */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL to the poster of the title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Date of birth of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the actor */,
  "height" TEXT /* Height of the actor */,
  "weight" TEXT /* Weight of the actor */,
  "biography" TEXT /* A brief biography of the actor */,
  "social_media" TEXT /* URL to the actor's social media profile */,
  "headshot_url" TEXT /* URL to the actor's headshot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "character_type" TEXT /* Type of character (e.g., main, supporting, guest) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the character */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the character */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the character */,
  "backstory" TEXT /* A brief backstory of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_characters" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the actors table */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the characters table */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* The date the actor started playing the character */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* The date the actor stopped playing the character (if applicable) */,
  "notable_scenes" TEXT /* A brief description of notable scenes the actor played in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id", "character_id", "title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "episodes" (
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each episode */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "episode_name" TEXT /* Name of the episode */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season number of the episode */,
  "episode_number" INTEGER /* Episode number of the episode */,
  "air_date" TEXT /* The date the episode was aired */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* The runtime of the episode */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the directors table */,
  "writer_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the writers table */,
  "summary" TEXT /* A brief summary of the episode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("episode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_episode_appearances" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the actors table */,
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the episodes table */,
  "role_type" TEXT /* The type of role the actor played in the episode (e.g., main, guest) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id", "episode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the actors table */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the award was won */,
  "category" TEXT /* The category of the award (e.g., best actor, best director) */,
  "awarding_body" TEXT /* The organization that awarded the award (e.g., Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "title_genres" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the genres table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "rating_name" TEXT /* Name of the rating */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "title_ratings" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the ratings table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id", "rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* The hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the title was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* The user agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1-5 stars) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* The date the review was posted */,
  "spoiler_flag" INTEGER /* A flag indicating whether the review contains spoilers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "watchlists" (
  "watchlist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each watchlist */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  "watchlist_name" TEXT /* Name of the watchlist */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time the watchlist was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time the watchlist was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("watchlist_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watchlists_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watchlists_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "watchlist_entries" (
  "watchlist_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the watchlists table */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the titles table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("watchlist_id", "title_id")
)
